focus: Inventory cross-encoder constants, provider ladder, cache TTL, and rerank thresholds
noveltyJustification: All three findings surfaced previously undocumented constant groupings for this packet and corrected the rerank control surface.

focus: Document Stage 1, Stage 2, and search-weights hardcoded values
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and one materially refined the packet framing around search-weights scope.

focus: Trace canonical decay math and real source-of-truth file layout
noveltyJustification: This pass corrected stale packet assumptions and tied FSRS constants to their canonical scheduler source.
ruledOut: Treating spec-listed Stage 1 and Stage 2 paths as authoritative

focus: Analyze adaptive fusion assembly and normalized intent-weight profiles
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted raw weight profiles into normalized continuity-relevant fusion guidance.

focus: Review RRF K-sensitivity analysis and intent-specific optimization hooks
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and one exposed a meaningful default-vs-baseline mismatch in the repo’s own eval tooling.

focus: Map fusion findings onto continuity-query priorities and config ownership
noveltyJustification: The new value came from synthesizing continuity-specific weight recommendations and separating vector-only from hybrid tuning.
ruledOut: Using search-weights.json as the primary continuity-tuning surface

focus: Profile provider contracts, fallback score bands, and fixture quality differences
noveltyJustification: Provider-quality deltas were new, while latency conclusions remained bounded by fixture-only evidence.
ruledOut: Claiming live provider latency distributions from mocked test fixtures

focus: Compare length-penalty thresholds against real spec-doc corpus shape and cache observability
noveltyJustification: The corpus-length probe and cache-observability gap both materially narrowed the remaining uncertainty.

focus: Synthesize threshold recommendations for continuity-first reranking
noveltyJustification: Mostly synthesis, but it resolved the practical threshold recommendations that earlier iterations only implied.

focus: Finalize cross-question recommendations and packet-local synthesis
noveltyJustification: The final pass was low-novelty synthesis that consolidated earlier evidence into implementation-ready guidance.

focus: Map length-penalty call graph, compatibility surface, and phase-001 blast radius
noveltyJustification: This pass translated the earlier removal recommendation into a concrete schema-to-runtime blast-radius map and showed that the safe first step is behavior removal with a compatibility no-op rather than immediate contract deletion.
ruledOut: Treating phase 001 as a one-line constant deletion with no compatibility work

focus: Audit penalty-specific tests and convert the removal decision into safe phase-001 implementation guidance
noveltyJustification: The new value came from identifying the exact suites and source-analysis assertions that currently pin the old thresholds and helper names, which turns phase 001 into a coordinated runtime-plus-test change instead of a one-file edit.
ruledOut: Replacing the deleted penalty with a higher threshold in phase 001

focus: Design reranker cache counters and identify the smallest canonical status surface for phase 002
noveltyJustification: This pass turned the earlier telemetry gap into a concrete counter model and established that the existing module-level status object is the right canonical source of truth for cache observability.
ruledOut: Adding counters directly to per-result payload rows

focus: Differentiate core reranker status changes from optional operator-visible telemetry exposure
noveltyJustification: Mostly a boundary-setting pass, but it resolved the important scope question: phase 002 can stay in cross-encoder.ts, while retrieval-telemetry mirroring is a separate follow-on rather than an implementation prerequisite.
ruledOut: Coupling phase 002 to TTL retuning or dashboard work

focus: Locate the minimum adaptive-fusion seam for a dedicated continuity profile
noveltyJustification: This pass showed that the adaptive fusion layer is already string-keyed, so a new continuity profile can land there without forcing an immediate classifier-wide refactor.
ruledOut: Treating continuity as a DEFAULT_WEIGHTS change instead of an explicit profile

focus: Trace the wider public-intent blast radius for continuity beyond adaptive-fusion.ts
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted a vague 'maybe broader' concern into a precise list of typed intent maps, routing helpers, and tests that would all need changes if continuity becomes a public search intent.
ruledOut: Assuming the MCP/tool surface will support continuity automatically once the profile exists

focus: Map Stage 3 rerank-minimum behavior and determine the safest first threshold
noveltyJustification: The pass separated Stage 3 policy from direct reranker behavior and showed that K-like caution applies here too: raising the threshold to 4 is a safer first move than jumping directly to 5 without candidate-size telemetry.
ruledOut: Changing the threshold inside cross-encoder.ts instead of Stage 3

focus: Audit Stage 3 regression fixtures for MIN_RESULTS_FOR_RERANK = 4 fallout
noveltyJustification: This pass narrowed the test fallout to a specific regression suite and showed that the broader reranker module tests can remain unchanged because they bypass the Stage 3 threshold entirely.
ruledOut: Mass-editing every reranker test file after the Stage 3 threshold change

focus: Choose the lowest-blast-radius harness for continuity-specific RRF K sweeps
noveltyJustification: The key new insight is that the repo already has two K-sweep paths with different coupling: the string-typed optimization harness can accept continuity immediately, while the typed judged sweep requires union expansion.
ruledOut: Starting with the typed judged sweep before the continuity intent/profile boundary is settled

focus: Synthesize the safe implementation map for phases 001-004 plus the continuity-specific K-sweep follow-up
noveltyJustification: Low-novelty synthesis pass that turns the resumed code sweep into an execution-ready dependency map: telemetry source of truth first, length-penalty behavior removal with compatibility guardrails, Stage 3 threshold to 4, continuity profile at an explicitly chosen scope, then K-sweep validation.
ruledOut: Treating all four implementation sub-phases as having the same kind of blast radius
